summary:
This joint resolution, enacted as Public Law 119-3, uses Congress's authority under the Congressional Review Act (chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code) to overturn a federal regulation.

Specifically, Congress disapproves the rule issued by the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management titled "Protection of Marine Archaeological Resources," which was published in the Federal Register on September 3, 2024 (89 Fed. Reg. 71160). Under the resolution, this rule "shall have no force or effect," meaning the regulation is nullified and cannot be implemented or enforced.

The Congressional Review Act provides a mechanism for Congress to review and reject rules issued by federal agencies. When a disapproval resolution such as this one is enacted, the targeted rule is treated as though it had never taken effect.

The resolution passed the Senate on February 25, 2025, passed the House on March 6, 2025, and was approved on March 14, 2025.
